---
title: "negative thinking reframe"
format: html
---

## Negative Thinking Reframe

Reframing means replacing a distorted thought with one that is more accurate, balanced, and actionable.

A strong reframe should lower emotional intensity and support your next healthy action.

Thought patterns shape emotion, motivation, and behavior. In high-stress moments, the mind often produces fast, extreme conclusions that feel true but are incomplete. These thoughts can intensify urges and narrow choices.

Learning to examine and reframe thoughts creates distance between impulse and action. Over time, this supports steadier mood, better decisions, and stronger follow-through on recovery goals.

::: callout
### Instructions
Use real examples from this week.
:::

| Negative thought | Evidence for | Evidence against | Balanced thought | Next action |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
